title: Rail Yard Engineers, Dinkey Operators, and Hostlers
description: Drive switching or other locomotive or dinkey engines within railroad yard, industrial plant, quarry, construction project, or similar location.

tasks.text: Observe and respond to wayside and cab signals, including color light signals, position signals, torpedoes, flags, and hot box detectors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect engines before and after use to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Apply and release hand brakes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Signal crew members for movement of engines or trains, using lanterns, hand signals, radios, or telephones.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with conductors and other workers via radiotelephones or computers to exchange switching information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect track for defects such as broken rails and switch malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Observe water levels and oil, air, and steam pressure gauges to ensure proper operation of equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Couple and uncouple air hoses and electrical connections between cars.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive engines within railroad yards or other establishments to couple, uncouple, or switch railroad cars.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect the condition of stationary trains, rolling stock, and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Read switching instructions and daily car schedules to determine work to be performed, or receive orders from yard conductors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Receive, relay, and act upon instructions and inquiries from train operations and customer service center personnel.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spot cars for loading and unloading at customer locations.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Operate track switches, derails, automatic switches, and retarders to change routing of train or cars.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report arrival and departure times, train delays, work order completion, and time on duty.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform routine repair and maintenance duties.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Drive locomotives to and from various stations in roundhouses to have locomotives cleaned, serviced, repaired, or supplied.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pull knuckles to open them for coupling.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide assistance in aligning drawbars, using available equipment to lift, pull, or push on the drawbars.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Ride on moving cars by holding onto grab irons and standing on ladder steps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record numbers of cars available, numbers of cars sent to repair stations, and types of service needed.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Operate flatcars equipped with derricks or railcars to transport personnel or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide assistance in the installation or repair of rails and ties.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
